LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SIMENSTAD MOTION FOR INJUNCTION TO PREVENT IRREPARABLE HARM FROM UNLAWFUL EXERCISE OF PUBLIC STATUTE AND OFFICE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR INJUNCTION DENIED. DEFENDANT DID HAVE PROPER STATUTORY AUTHORITY TO CONDUCT AN INVESTIGATION AND MAKE A RECOMMENDATION TO THE BOARD. (HARB. & NAV. SECTION 1180.3) SPEEDING CONSTITUTES MISCONDUCT SINCE HARB. & NAV. SEC. 1181 IS A NON-EXHAUSTIVE LIST OF MISCONDUCT FOR WHICH A LICENSE MAY BE SUSPENDED. NO EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT DEFENDANT'S INVESTIGATIONS WERE CONDUCTED IN ANY UNFAIR OR PARTIAL MANNER. PLAINTIFF CANNOT SHOW IRREPARABLE HARM OR A PROBABILITY PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR #12353
PROOF OF SERVICE OF OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER AND DECLARATION OF JOHN H. CIGAVIC III FILED BY PLAINTIFF SIMENSTAD, MICHAEL A CAPTAIN, A LICNESED SAN FRANCISCO BAR PILOT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA ACTING BY AND THROUGH THE BOARD OF PILOT COMMISSIONERS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT EXHAUSTED HIS ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-08-2010 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE OF CALIFORNIA ACTING BY AND THROUGH THE BOARD OF PILOT COMMISSIONERS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: John H. Cigavic, Esq. and George Wesley Nowell, Esq., Law Offices of George W. Nowell, (415) 362-1333 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Judith J. Loach, Deputy Attorney General, Dept. of Justice, (415) 703-5604 appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff has not exhausted his administrative remedies. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Injunction To Prevent Irreparable Harm From Unlawful Excercise Of Public Statute And Office;
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SIMENSTAD Motion for Injunction to Prevent Irreparable Harm from Unlawful Exercise of Public Statute and Office. Appearances: John H. Cigavic, Esq. and George Wesley Nowell, Esq., Law Offices of George W. Nowell, (415) 362-1333 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Judith J. Loach, Deputy Attorney General, Dept. of Justice, (415) 703-5604 appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for injunction denied. Defendant did have proper statutory authority to conduct an investigation and make a recommendation to the Board. (Harb. & Nav. section 1180.3) Speeding constitutes misconduct since Harb. & Nav. sec. 1181 is a non-exhaustive list of misconduct for which a license may be suspended. No evidence presented that defendant's investigations were conducted in any unfair or partial manner. Plaintiff cannot show irreparable harm or a probability prevailing on the merits. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR #12353
